channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Apple sues OpenAI for stealing trade secrets
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Apple has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI, accusing the AI company of stealing trade secrets and poaching employees to develop its own consumer hardware dev
Read More: https://punchng.com/apple-sues-openai-for-stealing-trade-secrets/

item.title: Tinubu approves upgrade of Nasarawa federal poly to specialised varsity
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
President Tinubu approves the upgrade of Federal Polytechnic, Nasarawa, to a specialised university focusing on mining, engineering, and technology.
Read More: https://punchng.com/tinubu-approves-upgrade-of-nasarawa-federal-poly-to-specialised-varsity/

item.title: DJ Chicken arraigned over threat to Seyi Tinubu, gets N1m bail
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Controversial DJ Chicken has been arraigned in Lagos for threatening Seyi Tinubu. He pleaded not guilty and was granted N1m bail.
Read More: https://punchng.com/dj-chicken-arraigned-over-threat-to-seyi-tinubu-gets-n1m-bail/

item.title: Police arrest suspected cultists, vandal in Abia
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Abia State Police Command has arrested four suspected cultists and one vandal, recovering firearms and other exhibits. Read more on the arrests in Abia.
Read More: https://punchng.com/police-arrest-suspected-cultists-vandal-in-abia/

item.title: P’Harcourt airport receives global certification, FAAN hails Fubara
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Port Harcourt International Airport achieves full global certification after 40 years, meeting international safety standards. Discover what this means for
Read More: https://punchng.com/pharcourt-airport-receives-global-certification-faan-hails-fubara/

item.title: (VIDEO) Airport clamping: Keyamo asks Obi to apologise, pay N25,000 fine
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Festus Keyamo demands Peter Obi apologize and pay a N25,000 fine for wrongful parking at Abuja airport, refuting Obi’s ‘persecution’ claims.
Read More: https://punchng.com/video-airport-clamping-keyamo-asks-obi-to-apologise-pay-n25000-fine/

item.title: ECB face no action over Stokes retirement video
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: No action will be taken by the International Cricket Council (ICC) over Ben Stokes' retirement video.

item.title: Madugu Names Strong Super Falcons Squad For WAFCON Title Defence
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Head Coach Justine Madugu has unveiled a 25-player Super Falcons squad led by captain Rasheedat Ajibade and goalkeeper Chiamaka Nnadozie as Nigeria prepares to defend its Women’s Africa Cup of Nations (WAFCON) title at the 2026 tournament in Morocco. The 14th edition of the competition will take pla

item.description:
Ogun State Governor Dapo Abiodun congratulates President Tinubu and security agencies on the successful rescue of kidnapped Oyo schoolchildren, praising th
Read More: https://punchng.com/abiodun-hails-tinubu-security-agencies-over-rescue-of-kidnapped-oyo-schoolchildren/

item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Nora Wittstruck, Chief Analytical Officer for Governments at S&P Global, joined Romaine Bostick and Katie Greifeld on Bloomberg's 'The Close' to provide insights on FIFA's financial position during the 2026 World Cup. She highlights how FIFA has emerged financially stronger despite facing numero

item.title: Mutfwang Commissions Potato Value Chain Projects, Distributes Agricultural Equipment
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Plateau State Governor, Barr. Caleb Manasseh Mutfwang on Friday commissioned the Potato Value Chain Project, a state-of-the-art Tissue Culture Laboratory and the distribution of modern agricultural equipment in Mangu Local Government Area. ‎ ‎The initiative is a landmark investment that will transfo

item.title: “Nigeria Has Never Experienced Advancements We’re Seeing Under Tinubu” — Ooni of Ife
item.dc:creator: Information Nigeria
item.description: The Ooni of Ife, Oba Adeyeye Enitan Ogunwusi, Ojaja II, has hailed President Bola Tinubu’s administration, saying Nigeria is witnessing unprecedented progress under his leadership. Speaking at the inauguration of the PBAT Door-to-Door Movement in Abuja on Thursday, the monarch commended Tinubu’s eco

item.title: Airtel Targets 5,000 Connected Schools As Poor Internet Access Threatens Learning
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Despite growing digital transformation across Africa, millions of schoolchildren, particularly in rural and underserved communities, remain cut off from quality online learning because of limited internet access and inadequate digital infrastructure. Against this backdrop, Airtel Africa has pledged

item.title: Jubilation in Ogbomoso as abducted schoolchildren, teachers rescued unharmed
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Jubilation in Ogbomoso as over 40 abducted schoolchildren and teachers are rescued unharmed. Read more about the successful rescue operation.
Read More: https://punchng.com/jubilation-in-ogbomoso-as-abducted-schoolchildren-teachers-rescued-unharmed/

item.title: Get Your PVCs Now To Determine Nigeria’s Future, NDC Urges Members, Nigerians
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Nigeria Democratic Congress (NDC), has called on all members of the party, supporters and every patriotic Nigerian to take full advantage of the ongoing Continuous Voter’s Registration(CVR) exercise and obtain their Permanent Voter Card (PVC). NDC National Publicity Secretary, Osa Director

item.title: Colombia’s Jaminton Campaz receives death threats after World Cup exit
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Campaz was threatened after failing to score in the last-16 match against Switzerland, which Colombia lost on penalties.

item.title: Pro Shop's Chad Mumm on 'Full Swing,' Golf in 2026
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Chad Mumm, Co-Founder of Pro Shop and Producer of the Netflix series 'Full Swing,' talks about the world's number-one ranked golfer, Scottie Scheffler, missing the cut at the Scottish Open. He also discusses golf's surge in popularity and the anticipation for the upcoming season of 'Full Swing.' Mum

item.title: Man fatally shot by ICE in Houston was not intended target, DHS says
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Immigration agents were looking for a different person when they shot Lorenzo Salgado Araujo during a traffic stop, officials say.

item.title: 1 Dies, 7 Injured As Gombe Assembly Complex Under Construction Collapses
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: One worker has been confirmed dead and seven others injured after a section of the New Gombe State House of Assembly Complex under construction collapsed on Friday. The Permanent Secretary of the Gombe State Ministry of Works, Housing and Transportation, Danladi Adams, said the incident occurred whe

item.title: BEYOND THE SHARE: WHY STRENGTHENING ELECTORAL INTEGRITY BEGINS WITH RESPONSIBLE COMMUNICATION
item.dc:creator: Guardian NG Opinion
item.description: By John Best Uche In an era where information travels faster than verification, the future of democracy may well depend on the choices we make before clicking the “Post” button. From 6–8 July 2026, I had the privilege of representing The post BEYOND THE SHARE: WHY STRENGTHENING ELECTORAL

item.title: Under Lula, Amazon deforestation falls to lowest level in a decade
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The figures come months before Brazil’s presidential election, with Lula promising to end illegal deforestation by 2030.

item.title: The FCC is cracking down on DJI tech that dodged the foreign drone ban
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: Last year, we told you about Xtra, the company that lets DJI sneak its popular cameras into the US, and Skyrover, a brand seemingly selling DJI drones in disguise. They're just two of the many firms DJI is suspected of starting to skirt the United States' foreign drone ban. But it appears the FCC is

item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Investors are bullish on Meta’s new low-cost AI pricing and infrastructure plans.

item.title: Oyo abduction: No ransom paid, no concession made – Tinubu
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
President Tinubu confirms no ransom was paid for the release of Oyo abducted pupils and teachers, praising security forces for their successful operation.

item.dc:creator: New Telegraph

The group described the move as a demonstration of continuity, stability, and commitment to nationa

item.title: World Cup: Newly discovered marine species named after Cape Verde’s Vozinha
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Cape Verde goalkeeper Vozinha has received an extraordinary honour after a newly discovered marine species was named after him in recognition of his remarkable performances at the 2026 FIFA World Cup. item.title: SK Hynix’s stock sees double-digit pop in Nasdaq debut
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: The South Korean memory-chip company’s new American depositary receipts began trading on Friday and logged gains of nearly 13%.

item.title: 2 Die As Flood Submerges Homes, Displaces Residents In Akwa Ibom
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: At least two persons have died after two days of torrential rainfall triggered devastating floods that submerged homes, displaced residents and crippled movement across Uyo, the Akwa Ibom State capital, and parts of Itu Local Government Area. The victims were reportedly swept away by floodwaters alo

item.title: NYSC: Rep Writes Tinubu, Says Reforms Require More Input From Stakeholders
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: A member of the House of Representatives, Hon. Philip Agbese, has called for the suspension of the sweeping reforms to the National Youth Service Corps (NYSC) recently approved by the Federal Executive Council (FEC). Agbese, representing Ado/Okpokwu/Ogbadibo Federal Constituency and Deputy Spokesman

item.title: Canada’s Bill C-36 tackles AI privacy. Is it enough?
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Canada’s new privacy bill promises stronger protections for children, but experts say it still misses risks in the AI world.

item.dc:creator: MarketWatch

item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Midfielder Declan Rice and defenders Marc Guehi and Reece James all train with England on the eve of Saturday's World Cup quarter-final against Norway.

In a statement mad

item.title: Spain into semi-finals at expense of Belgium after Merino's late winner
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Mikel Merino emerges as Spain's hero yet again as the substitute scores in the 88th minute to stun Belgium and set up a World Cup semi-final against France.

item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Bloomberg Money takes the pulse of your financial life, powered by the reporting of our global newsroom. Today's guests are: University of Chicago Booth School Professor of Economics and Former Fed Governor Randall Kroszner and NewEdge Wealth CIO Cameron Dawson. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: Sinner Sweeps Djokovic In Straight Sets To Reach Wimbledon Final
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Defending champion Jannik Sinner produced a flawless performance Friday, dispatching Novak Djokovic 6-4, 6-4, 6-4 to advance to his second consecutive Wimbledon final, where he will face Alexander Zverev. The world number one denied the 39-year-old Djokovic any break-point opportunities until the th

item.title: Obi not bigger than law, must apologise, pay N25,000 fine for wrong parking — Keyamo

item.title: SK Hynix Debut Is a Bet That AI Breaks Boom-and-Bust Chip Cycle
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: South Korean memory chipmaker SK Hynix Inc. just pulled off the largest public listing by a foreign company in US market history. Shares soared 13% on their first day of trading.

item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: Schneider, who formerly served as the CEO of Automattic and is a partner at True Ventures, says he is "all in" on the unconventional social media platform.

item.title: World Cup 2026: Merino Strikes Late Again As Spain Edge Out Belgium, Set Up Semifinal Clash With France
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Arsenal star Mikel Merino was the match winner once again as his late strike for Spain helped them to a 2-1 win against Belgium in the FIFA World Cup 2026 quarterfinal on Friday. With two minutes left in the game Merino, who was the match winner in the round of 16 against Portugal, pounced on [...]

item.title: Nuclear Energy Services Firm Holtec Joins Sector’s IPO Rush
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Holtec Nuclear Corp. filed for a US initial public offering, becoming the latest in the sector to try to capitalize on data centers’ growing power demands.

item.title: Rhode Island Jail Operator Files Bankruptcy to Cut Bond Debt
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Central Falls Detention Facility Corp., which houses federal detainees including people arrested by the US Immigration and Customs Enforcement agency, filed for bankruptcy saying it cannot afford to keep paying on more than $167 million in bond debt.

item.description: The plant-eating dinosaur, named Uragasaurus kalasinensis, is thought to have lived about 150 million years ago.

item.description: Spain scored a late winner to defeat Belgium 2-1 and progress into the semi-final of the 2026 FIFA World Cup on Friday. item.title: Governors Blamed For Insecurity Without Powers, Says Makinde
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: Makinde recalled that former President Goodluck Jonathan was blamed for the Chibok schoolgirls' abduction, arguing that governors now face similar criticism over insecurity in their states despite lacking control over security agencies. The post Governors Blamed For Insecurity Without Powers, Says M

item.title: My insurance company said my roof lost a few tiles. Loss adjusters found $10,000 in storm damage. How could this happen?
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: “My house shook violently from the wind.”

item.title: Access Holdings hands N5.5bn equity stake to 77 employees
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: Access Holdings Plc has officially vested over 220 million ordinary shares valued at approximately N5.5 billion to 77 key employees read more Access Holdings hands N5.5bn equity stake to 77 employees

item.title: Spain Defeat Belgium 2-1 To Reach World Cup Semi-Finals
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: Spain will now face France at the AT&T Stadium in Arlington, Texas on Tuesday. The post Spain Defeat Belgium 2-1 To Reach World Cup Semi-Finals appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: Merino strikes late as Spain edge Belgium to book World Cup semi-final spot
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Merino's latest heroics continued his remarkable knack for delivering in decisive moments. The midfielder also came off the bench to score the stoppage-time winner that eliminated Portugal in the Round of 16, and once again proved to be Spain's match-winner when the stakes were highest.

item.title: ‘We are not a tribal club’ — Enugu Rangers reject Moses Simon’s discrimination claim.
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: "For over five decades, Rangers has stood as one of Nigeria's foremost national football institutions. Although proudly rooted in Enugu and the South-East, the club has always belonged to Nigeria. The post ‘We are not a tribal club’ — Enugu Rangers reject Moses Simon’s discriminati

item.title: NDPHC hands over Gbarain Power Plant rehabilitation project to TILT/Schneider Electric JV
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Niger Delta Power Holding Company (NDPHC) has officially handed over the rehabilitation of its Gbarain Power Plant to a joint venture comprising TILT Energy Limited and Schneider Electric to replace the plant’s burnt Power Control Module (PCM), marking a significant milestone in efforts to

item.title: ‘Day of thanksgiving’, Ajadi lauds rescue of abducted Oyo pupils, teachers
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Allied Peoples Movement (APM) senatorial candidate for Oyo Central Senatorial District, Ambassador Olufemi Ajadi Oguntoyinbo, has described the rescue of the abducted pupils and teachers from Oriire Local Government Area of Oyo State as “a day of thanksgiving,” congratulating Governor Se

item.title: Prismata: Confining cross-site prompt injection in web agents
item.dc:creator: Hacker News
item.description: Article URL: https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.08147 Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48865238 Points: 9 # Comments: 0

item.title: Gov Lawal: State Police Will Empower Governors to Tackle Insecurity
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: Zamfara State Governor, Dauda Lawal, said state police will go a long way toward tackling insecurity in the country by giving governors real authority as chief security officers of their states. He made the remarks as a panellist at the Arise News Town Hall Summit: Building a National Consensus for

item.title: World Cup 2026: Merino strikes late again as Spain beat Belgium to set up France semi-final
item.dc:creator: France 24

item.title: Dozens of abducted schoolchildren and teachers rescued in Nigeria
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The hostages were taken from three separate schools in the southwestern state of Oyo in May.

item.title: Enugu Rangers Refused To Sign Me Because I Was Not Igbo —Super Eagles Star Simon Claims
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Super Eagles and Paris FC winger Moses Simon has alleged that he was denied the opportunity to sign for Enugu Rangers early in his football career because he was not igbo. Simon, who made the claim in an interview with UrbanDwellSport, revealed that he spent three months training with the Flying Ant

item.title: Tinubu Okays Upgrade Of Nasarawa Poly To Specialised Varsity
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: President Bola Tinubu has approved the upgrade of the Federal Polytechnic, Nasarawa, into the Federal University of Mining, Engineering and Technology, Nasarawa. The upgrade was in line with President Tinubu’s commitment to strengthening technical education, developing human capital, and unlocking N

item.title: Apple sues OpenAI over alleged trade secret theft
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: Apple alleges the misconduct was directed by OpenAi's senior leadership, including a long-time former employee.

item.title: Nigeria LNG clinches top operational excellence award
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: By Doris Obinna Nigeria LNG Limited (NLNG) has won the Operational Excellence Award at the 2026 Energy Awards, a recognition of its performance in safety, efficiency and reliability at a time when the company is advancing its Train 7 expansion project. The award was presented during the 25th anniver

item.title: 片山氏のGPIF発言、全東信の破産ショック、物価の権威が利上げ予測─1週間のニュース5選
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: マーケットで話題になったニュースから、1週間を振り返る五つのトピックを厳選して紹介します。
